---
title: Effects Bottleneck Size on Feature Importance 
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.append('../')
```

```{python}
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.optim import AdamW

from diomics.models.multi_mlp import simple_FC, JointMLP
from diomics.featimportance.shapley import JointMLPWrapper
from diomics.featimportance.igrads import integrated_grads
from diomics.featimportance.comparison import overlap_sets
from diomics.featimportance.plot_helpers import igrad_beeswarm_plot, make_igrad_plot_df, facet_force_plots
import shap

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import copy
import datetime
import re
from tqdm.notebook import tqdm

from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve, auc, confusion_matrix, v_measure_score, roc_auc_score
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans, AgglomerativeClustering
from scipy.stats import weightedtau

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.cm as cm
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
import seaborn as sns

import pickle
```

## Load ICL-104

```{python}
proData = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/pro_edata_mlready.csv')
lipData = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/lip_edata_mlready.csv')
metabData = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/metab_edata_mlready.csv')

pro_fdata = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/pro_fdata_mlready.csv')
lip_fdata = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/lip_fdata_mlready.csv')
metab_fdata = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/metab_fdata_mlready.csv')

lipData.shape, metabData.shape, proData.shape
```

```{python}
train_lip = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/lip_neg_train.csv')
test_lip = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/lip_neg_test.csv')

train_metab = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/metab_train.csv')
test_metab = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/metab_test.csv')

train_pro = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/pro_train.csv')
test_pro = pd.read_csv('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/data/ICL104-binary/pro_test.csv')

ytrain = train_lip['y']
ytest = test_lip['y']

train_lip = train_lip.set_index("SampleID").drop("y", axis=1)
test_lip = test_lip.set_index("SampleID").drop("y", axis=1)

train_metab = train_metab.set_index("SampleID").drop("y", axis=1)
test_metab = test_metab.set_index("SampleID").drop("y", axis=1)

train_pro = train_pro.set_index("SampleID").drop("y", axis=1)
test_pro = test_pro.set_index("SampleID").drop("y", axis=1)
```

```{python}
# 0-1 normalize everything
# test_lip = (test_lip - np.min(test_lip)) / (np.max(test_lip) - np.min(test_lip))
# train_lip = (train_lip - np.min(train_lip)) / (np.max(train_lip) - np.min(train_lip))

# test_metab = (test_metab - np.min(test_metab)) / (np.max(test_metab) - np.min(test_metab))
# train_metab = (train_metab - np.min(train_metab)) / (np.max(train_metab) - np.min(train_metab))

# test_pro = (test_pro - np.min(test_pro)) / (np.max(test_pro) - np.min(test_pro))
# train_pro = (train_pro - np.min(train_pro)) / (np.max(train_pro) - np.min(train_pro))

# standard normalize everything using the statistics from the training data
# test_lip = (test_lip - train_lip.mean())/train_lip.std()
# train_lip = (train_lip - train_lip.mean())/train_lip.std()

# test_metab = (test_metab - train_metab.mean())/train_metab.std()
# train_metab = (train_metab - train_metab.mean())/train_metab.std()

# test_pro = (test_pro - train_pro.mean())/train_pro.std()
# train_pro = (train_pro - train_pro.mean())/train_pro.std()

test_lip = test_lip - test_lip.median().median()
train_lip = train_lip - train_lip.median().median()

test_metab = test_metab - test_metab.median().median()
train_metab = train_metab - train_metab.median().median()

test_pro = test_pro - test_pro.median().median()
train_pro = train_pro - train_pro.median().median()
```

```{python}
# Add noise features to each dataset
n_noise = 100

lip_stds = train_lip.std().sample(n_noise)
metab_stds = train_metab.std().sample(n_noise)
pro_stds = train_pro.std().sample(n_noise)

lip_means = train_lip.mean().sample(n_noise)
metab_means = train_metab.mean().sample(n_noise)
pro_means = train_pro.mean().sample(n_noise)

train_lip_noise = np.random.normal(lip_means, lip_stds, (train_lip.shape[0], n_noise))
train_metab_noise = np.random.normal(metab_means, metab_stds, (train_metab.shape[0], n_noise))
train_pro_noise = np.random.normal(pro_means, pro_stds, (train_pro.shape[0], n_noise))

train_lip_copy = train_lip.copy()
train_metab_copy = train_metab.copy()
train_pro_copy = train_pro.copy()

# attach the noise to the copies
train_lip_copy = pd.concat([train_lip_copy, pd.DataFrame(train_lip_noise, index = train_lip_copy.index)], axis = 1)
train_metab_copy = pd.concat([train_metab_copy, pd.DataFrame(train_metab_noise, index = train_metab_copy.index)], axis = 1)
train_pro_copy = pd.concat([train_pro_copy, pd.DataFrame(train_pro_noise, index = train_pro_copy.index)], axis = 1)
```

### First model with just Metabolites + Proteins

```{python}
# turn data into tensors
tensor_1 = torch.tensor(train_metab.values, dtype = torch.float32)
tensor_2 = torch.tensor(train_pro.values, dtype = torch.float32)
tensor_3 = torch.tensor(train_metab_copy.values, dtype = torch.float32)

views_orig = [tensor_1, tensor_2]
views_aug = [tensor_3, tensor_2]

y_gt = ytrain.astype('category').cat.codes
y_gt = torch.tensor(y_gt.values, dtype=torch.int64)

cat_names = ytrain.astype('category').cat.categories

test_x = [test_metab, test_pro]
test_y = ytest
```

```{python}
train_acc = []
test_acc = []

# hyperparameters
n_iters = 1250
# class weights, data are balanced
alpha = None
gamma = 3

# control the contribution of the marginal loss
marginal_weight = 1

# make a coefficient for the marginal losses proportional to the number of features of the corresponding dataset
# marginal_coefs = [1/views[i].shape[1] for i in range(3)]
# marginal_coefs = [marginal_coefs[i]/sum(marginal_coefs) for i in range(3)]
marginal_coefs = [1, 1]
```

```{python}
# three view-specific encoders with the appropriate input size:

# the last hidden size must be the same since we are averaging
last_hidden_size = 64
dropout = 0.2

# model using un-augmented data
marg_1 = simple_FC(input_size=views_orig[0].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)
marg_2 = simple_FC(input_size=views_orig[1].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)
joint_model_orig = JointMLP(marginal_models=[marg_1, marg_2], hidden_dim=64, dropout=dropout)

optimizer_orig = AdamW(joint_model_orig.parameters(), lr = 1e-4)

# model using augmented data
marg_1 = simple_FC(input_size=views_aug[0].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)
marg_2 = simple_FC(input_size=views_aug[1].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)

joint_model_aug = JointMLP(marginal_models=[marg_1, marg_2], hidden_dim=64, dropout=dropout)

optimizer_aug = AdamW(joint_model_aug.parameters(), lr = 1e-4)

# another model for the original dataset to compare the effect of random initialization
marg_1 = simple_FC(input_size=views_orig[0].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)
marg_2 = simple_FC(input_size=views_orig[1].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)

joint_model_orig_rand = JointMLP(marginal_models=[marg_1, marg_2], hidden_dim=64, dropout=dropout)
optimizer_orig_rand = AdamW(joint_model_orig_rand.parameters(), lr = 1e-4)
```

Set up training functions
```{python}
def train(joint_model, optimizer, views_orig, y_gt, n_iters = 1000, alpha = None, gamma = 3, prog = True):
    train_acc = []
    test_acc = []

    if prog:
        pbar = tqdm(range(n_iters))
    else:
        pbar = range(n_iters)

    for i in pbar:
        # update the mlp
        yhat, h, yhats, hiddens = joint_model(*views_orig)

        # pass the predictions and distributions to the loss function and update parameters
        product_loss, marginal_losses, loss = joint_model.loss(y_gt, yhat, yhats, alpha = alpha, gamma = gamma)

        optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()

        optimizer.step()

        train_acc.append((yhat.argmax(dim=1).numpy() == y_gt.numpy()).mean())

        if (i+1) % 10 == 0 and prog:
            # update the tqdm message
            pbar.set_postfix({'train_acc': train_acc[-1], 'loss': loss.item()})
            # print(f'Iteration {i+1} loss: {loss.item():.3f}')

    return joint_model, train_acc, test_acc

def evaluate_overlap(shaps_1, shaps_2, n_features = 10):
    # get the set intersection of the above
    overlaps = set((-shaps_1).argsort()[:n_features]).intersection(set((-shaps_2).argsort()[:n_features]))
    tau, _ = weightedtau(shaps_1, shaps_2)

    return overlaps, tau

def make_joint_model(input_size, hidden_sizes=[[128, 64, 64]]*2, dropout=0.2, **kwargs):
    marg_1 = simple_FC(input_size=input_size[0], hidden_sizes=hidden_sizes[0], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)
    marg_2 = simple_FC(input_size=input_size[1], hidden_sizes=hidden_sizes[1], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)

    joint_model = JointMLP(marginal_models=[marg_1, marg_2], hidden_dim=64, dropout=dropout, **kwargs)
    optimizer = AdamW(joint_model.parameters(), lr = 1e-4)

    return joint_model, optimizer

def compare_shap_scores(
    data_1, 
    data_2, 
    y, 
    test_x,
    test_y,
    noise_levels=[100], 
    hidden_sizes = [[128, 64, 64], [128, 64, 64]], 
    n_evals_per_noise=10, 
    prog=True,
    **kwargs):
    
    results = {}

    for n_noise, hs in zip(noise_levels, hidden_sizes):
        # Add noise features to each dataset
        stds_1 = data_1.std().sample(n_noise, replace = True)
        stds_2 = data_2.std().sample(n_noise, replace = True)

        means_1 = data_1.mean().sample(n_noise, replace = True)
        means_2 = data_2.mean().sample(n_noise, replace = True)

        data_1_noise = np.random.normal(means_1, stds_1, (data_1.shape[0], n_noise))
        data_2_noise = np.random.normal(means_2, stds_2, (data_2.shape[0], n_noise))

        data_1_noise_test = np.random.normal(means_1, stds_1, (test_x[0].shape[0], n_noise))

        test_x_aug = [
            pd.concat([test_x[0], pd.DataFrame(data_1_noise_test, index = test_x[0].index)], axis = 1),
            test_x[1]
        ]

        test_tensors = [torch.tensor(test_x[0].values, dtype = torch.float32), torch.tensor(test_x[1].values, dtype = torch.float)]
        test_tensors_aug = [torch.tensor(test_x_aug[0].values, dtype = torch.float32), torch.tensor(test_x_aug[1].values, dtype = torch.float32)]

        data_1_copy = data_1.copy()
        # data_2_copy = data_2.copy()

        # attach the noise to the copies
        data_1_copy = pd.concat([data_1_copy, pd.DataFrame(data_1_noise, index = data_1_copy.index)], axis = 1)
        # data_2_copy = pd.concat([data_2_copy, pd.DataFrame(data_2_noise, index = data_2_copy.index)], axis = 1)

        tensor_1 = torch.tensor(data_1.values, dtype = torch.float32)
        tensor_2 = torch.tensor(data_2.values, dtype = torch.float32)
        tensor_3 = torch.tensor(data_1_copy.values, dtype = torch.float32)

        views_orig = [tensor_1, tensor_2]
        views_aug = [tensor_3, tensor_2]
        
        y_gt = y.astype('category').cat.codes
        y_gt = torch.tensor(y_gt.values, dtype=torch.int64)

        by_model_results = {}

        for i in range(n_evals_per_noise):
            joint_model_orig, optimizer_orig = make_joint_model([views_orig[0].shape[1], views_orig[1].shape[1]], hs, 0.2, **kwargs)
            joint_model_aug, optimizer_aug = make_joint_model([views_aug[0].shape[1], views_aug[1].shape[1]], hs, 0.2, **kwargs)
            joint_model_orig_rand, optimizer_orig_rand = make_joint_model([views_orig[0].shape[1], views_orig[1].shape[1]], hs, 0.2, **kwargs)

            joint_model_orig, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model_orig, optimizer_orig, views_orig, y_gt, n_iters = 1250, alpha = None, gamma = 3, prog=prog)
            joint_model_aug, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model_aug, optimizer_aug, views_aug, y_gt, n_iters = 1250, alpha = None, gamma = 3, prog=prog)
            joint_model_orig_rand, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model_orig_rand, optimizer_orig_rand, views_orig, y_gt, n_iters = 1250, alpha = None, gamma = 3, prog=prog)

            # eval all models
            joint_model_orig.eval()
            joint_model_aug.eval()
            joint_model_orig_rand.eval()

            joint_model_orig_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model_orig)
            explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_orig_wrp, views_orig)
            shap_values_orig = explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False)

            joint_model_aug_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model_aug)
            explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_aug_wrp, views_aug)
            shap_values_aug = explainer.shap_values(views_aug, check_additivity=False)

            joint_model_orig_rand_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model_orig_rand)
            explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_orig_rand_wrp, views_orig)
            shap_values_orig_rand = explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False)

            scores_1_1 = np.abs(shap_values_orig[0][0]).mean(axis=0)
            scores_2_1 = np.abs(shap_values_aug[0][0]).mean(axis=0)

            # remove the noise features
            scores_2_1_trunc = scores_2_1[:data_1.shape[1]]

            scores_3_1 = np.abs(shap_values_orig_rand[0][0]).mean(axis=0)

            scores_1_2 = np.abs(shap_values_orig[0][1]).mean(axis=0)
            scores_2_2 = np.abs(shap_values_aug[0][1]).mean(axis=0)
            scores_3_2 = np.abs(shap_values_orig_rand[0][1]).mean(axis=0)

            scores_1_all = np.concatenate([scores_1_1, scores_1_2])
            scores_2_all = np.concatenate([scores_2_1_trunc, scores_2_2])
            scores_3_all = np.concatenate([scores_3_1, scores_3_2])

            # we expect that the overlap between the scores_1 and scores_2 to be lower than that of scores_1 and scores_3
            # scores_1 = original # scores_2 = augmented # scores_3 = random initialization
            overlap_1_2, tau_1_2 = evaluate_overlap(scores_1_all, scores_2_all)
            overlap_1_3, tau_1_3 = evaluate_overlap(scores_1_all, scores_3_all)
            overlap_2_3, tau_2_3 = evaluate_overlap(scores_2_all, scores_3_all)

            y_gt_test = test_y.astype('category').cat.codes
            y_gt_test = torch.tensor(y_gt_test.values, dtype=torch.int64)

            # get test ROC for the original and augmented model:

            # original data score
            yhat, _, _, _ = joint_model_orig(*test_tensors)

            logits = yhat.detach().numpy()
            
            if logits.shape[1] == 2:
                logits = logits[:,1]

            auc_orig = roc_auc_score(y_gt_test.numpy(), logits, multi_class = 'ovr')
            
            # augmented data score
            yhat, _, _, _ = joint_model_aug(*test_tensors_aug)

            logits = yhat.detach().numpy()
            
            if logits.shape[1] == 2:
                logits = logits[:,1]

            auc_aug = roc_auc_score(y_gt_test.numpy(), logits, multi_class = 'ovr')

            by_model_results.setdefault('auc_orig', []).append(auc_orig)
            by_model_results.setdefault('auc_aug', []).append(auc_aug)
            by_model_results.setdefault('results_1_2', []).append((overlap_1_2, tau_1_2))
            by_model_results.setdefault('results_1_3', []).append((overlap_1_3, tau_1_3))
            by_model_results.setdefault('results_2_3', []).append((overlap_2_3, tau_2_3))
            by_model_results.setdefault('shap_values_orig', []).append(shap_values_orig)
            by_model_results.setdefault('shap_values_aug', []).append(shap_values_aug)
            by_model_results.setdefault('shap_values_orig_rand', []).append(shap_values_orig_rand)

        results[(n_noise, str(hs))] = by_model_results
            
    return results

def collect_results(comparisons):
    tau_scores = {k: {} for k in comparisons.keys()}
    shap_mags = {k: {} for k in comparisons.keys()}

    for k, comp in comparisons.items():
        tau_scores[k]['tau_1_2'] = [el[1] for el in comp['results_1_2']]
        tau_scores[k]['tau_1_3'] = [el[1] for el in comp['results_1_3']]

        shap_mags[k]['shap_mag_orig'] = [np.abs(el[0][0]).mean(axis=0) for el in comp['shap_values_orig']]
        shap_mags[k]['shap_mag_aug'] = [np.abs(el[0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]).mean(axis=0) for el in comp['shap_values_aug']]
        shap_mags[k]['shap_mag_orig_2'] = [np.abs(el[0][1]).mean(axis=0) for el in comp['shap_values_orig']]

    return tau_scores, shap_mags

def plot_tau(tau_dict, ax):
    all_tau_v_noise = []

    for k, v in tau_dict.items():
        all_tau_v_noise += v['tau_1_3']

    all_data = [all_tau_v_noise]

    for k, v in tau_dict.items():
        all_data.append(v['tau_1_2'])

    # make the color the same across all boxplots
    sns.boxplot(data = all_data, ax = ax, color = 'skyblue')
    ax.set_xticklabels([0] + list(tau_dict.keys()))
    ax.set_xlabel('Number of Noise Features')
    ax.set_ylabel('Weighted Tau')
    
    # rotate x axis labels
    ax.set_xticklabels(ax.get_xticklabels(), rotation = 90)
```

```{python}
# train each model
joint_model_orig, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model_orig, optimizer_orig, views_orig, y_gt, n_iters = n_iters, alpha = None, gamma = 3)
joint_model_aug, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model_aug, optimizer_aug, views_aug, y_gt, n_iters = n_iters, alpha = None, gamma = 3)
joint_model_orig_rand, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model_orig_rand, optimizer_orig_rand, views_orig, y_gt, n_iters = n_iters, alpha = None, gamma = 3)
```

```{python}
# shap values for original data
baselines = views_orig
joint_model_orig_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model_orig)
explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_orig_wrp, baselines)
shap_values_orig = explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False)

# shap values for augmented data
baselines = views_aug
joint_model_aug_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model_aug)
explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_aug_wrp, baselines)
shap_values_aug = explainer.shap_values(views_aug, check_additivity=False)

# shap values for the second original model
baselines = views_orig
joint_model_orig_rand_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model_orig_rand)
explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_orig_rand_wrp, baselines)
shap_values_orig_rand = explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False)
```

```{python}
scores_1 = np.abs(shap_values_orig[0][0]).mean(axis=0)
scores_2 = np.abs(shap_values_aug[0][0]).mean(axis=0)

scores_1_prot = np.abs(shap_values_orig[0][1]).mean(axis=0)
scores_2_prot = np.abs(shap_values_aug[0][1]).mean(axis=0)

scores_1.argsort()[:20]
scores_2.argsort()[:20]

weightedtau(scores_1, scores_2)

# remove the noise features
scores_2_trunc = scores_2[:train_metab.shape[1]]

# concatenate with the protein scores
scores_2_all = np.concatenate([scores_2_trunc, scores_2_prot])
scores_1_all = np.concatenate([scores_1, scores_1_prot])

weightedtau(scores_1_all, scores_2_all)

scores_1_all.argsort()[:40]
scores_2_all.argsort()[:40]

# get the set intersection of the above
set((-scores_1_all).argsort()[:10]).intersection(set((-scores_2_all).argsort()[:10]))

scores_3 = np.abs(shap_values_orig_rand[0][0]).mean(axis=0)
scores_3_prot = np.abs(shap_values_orig_rand[0][1]).mean(axis=0)
scores_3_all = np.concatenate([scores_3, scores_3_prot])

evaluate_overlap(scores_1_all, scores_3_all)
evaluate_overlap(scores_1_all, scores_2_all)
```

```{python}
# compute the shapley values
# [lip_baseline, metab_baseline, pro_baseline]
# shap_values_orig = explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False)

# shap_save = shap_values_orig

scores_1 = np.abs(shap_values_orig[0][0]).mean(axis=0)
scores_2 = np.abs(shap_save[0][0]).mean(axis=0)

scores_1.argsort()[:20]
scores_2.argsort()[:20]

weightedtau(scores_1, scores_2)
```

## Manual Runs over different noise levels and random initializations

```{python}
results = compare_shap_scores(train_metab, train_pro, ytrain, noise_levels=[10, 100, 500, 1000, 5000], n_evals_per_noise=5)
```

```{python}
results = compare_shap_scores(train_metab, train_pro, ytrain, noise_levels=[50, 5000], n_evals_per_noise=10)
```

```{python}
pickle.dump(results, open("/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/noise_level_results_50_5000.pkl", 'wb'))
```

```{python}
pickle.dump(results, open("/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/noise_level_results.pkl", 'wb'))
```

```{python}
results_orig = pickle.load(open("/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/noise_level_results.pkl", 'rb'))
results_50_5000 = pickle.load(open("/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/noise_level_results_50_5000.pkl", 'rb'))

# combine these two dictionaries
results = {**results_orig, **results_50_5000}
```

```{python}
tau_50_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[50]['results_1_2']]
tau_50_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[50]['results_1_3']]

tau_100_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[100]['results_1_2']]
tau_100_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[100]['results_1_3']]

tau_500_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[500]['results_1_2']]
tau_500_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[500]['results_1_3']]

tau_1000_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[1000]['results_1_2']]
tau_1000_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[1000]['results_1_3']]

tau_5000_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[5000]['results_1_2']]
tau_5000_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[5000]['results_1_3']]

# the average magnitude of the shapley values from the original model
shap_mag_50_orig = [np.abs(results[50]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[50]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_50_aug = [np.abs(results[50]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[50]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_100_orig = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_100_aug = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_500_orig = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_500_aug = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_1000_orig = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_1000_aug = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_5000_orig = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_5000_aug = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_50_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[50]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[50]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_50_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[50]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[50]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_100_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_100_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_500_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_500_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_1000_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_1000_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_5000_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_5000_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_aug']))]
```

```{python}
all_tau_v_noise = tau_50_1_3 + tau_100_1_3 + tau_500_1_3 + tau_1000_1_3 + tau_5000_1_3

# make some boxplots of the tau values:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize = (10, 6))

# make the color the same across all boxplots
sns.boxplot(data = [all_tau_v_noise, tau_50_1_2, tau_100_1_2, tau_500_1_2, tau_1000_1_2, tau_5000_1_2], ax = ax, color = 'skyblue')
ax.set_xticklabels([0, 50, 100, 500, 1000, 5000])
ax.set_xlabel('Number of Noise Features')
ax.set_ylabel('Weighted Tau')

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

fig.savefig('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/images/tau_v_noise.png', dpi = 300)
```

```{python}
# make boxplots of the absolute magnitude of the shapley values

vals_50_orig = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_50_orig]
vals_100_orig = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_100_orig]
vals_500_orig = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_500_orig]
vals_1000_orig = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_1000_orig]
vals_5000_orig = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_5000_orig]

vals_50_aug = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_50_aug]
vals_100_aug = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_100_aug]
vals_500_aug = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_500_aug]
vals_1000_aug = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_1000_aug]
vals_5000_aug = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_5000_aug]

vals_50_orig_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_50_orig_2]
vals_100_orig_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_100_orig_2]
vals_500_orig_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_500_orig_2]
vals_1000_orig_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_1000_orig_2]
vals_5000_orig_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_5000_orig_2]

vals_50_aug_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_50_aug_2]
vals_100_aug_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_100_aug_2]
vals_500_aug_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_500_aug_2]
vals_1000_aug_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_1000_aug_2]
vals_5000_aug_2 = [sv.mean() for sv in shap_mag_5000_aug_2]

# grouped boxplot orig vs aug with n_noise as the x-axis
all_orig_vals = vals_50_orig + vals_100_orig + vals_500_orig + vals_1000_orig + vals_5000_orig
all_orig_vals_2 = vals_50_orig_2 + vals_100_orig_2 + vals_500_orig_2 + vals_1000_orig_2 + vals_5000_orig_2

shap_mags = all_orig_vals + vals_50_aug + vals_100_aug + vals_500_aug + vals_1000_aug + vals_5000_aug
shap_mags_2 = all_orig_vals_2 + vals_50_aug_2 + vals_100_aug_2 + vals_500_aug_2 + vals_1000_aug_2 + vals_5000_aug_2

# all_orig_vals has 50 values, each of the augmentation levels has 10
n_noise_features = [0]*50 + [50]*10 + [100]*10 + [500]*10 + [1000]*10 + [5000]*10

#  concatenate shap_mags
plot_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Shapley Magnitude': shap_mags,
    'Shapeley Magnitude (Proteins)': shap_mags_2,
    'Shapley Ratio': [shap_mags[i]/shap_mags_2[i] for i in range(len(shap_mags))],
    'N Noise Features': n_noise_features,
})

```

```{python}
# plot of ratio between the metabolite and protein shapley values
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize = (10, 6))

sns.boxplot(data = plot_df, x = 'N Noise Features', y = 'Shapley Ratio', ax = ax, color = 'skyblue')

ax.set_xlabel('Number of Noise Features')
ax.set_ylabel('Shapley Ratio')

fig.tight_layout()

plt.show()

fig.savefig('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/images/shap_ratio_v_noise.png', dpi = 300)
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize = (10, 6))

sns.boxplot(data = plot_df, x = 'N Noise Features', y = 'Shapley Magnitude', ax = ax, color = 'skyblue')
ax.set_xlabel('Number of Noise Features')
ax.set_ylabel('Shapley Magnitude')

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

fig.savefig('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/images/shap_mag_v_noise.png', dpi = 300)
```

```{python}
# make a table of the tau scores and shapley magnitudes
tau_scores = pd.DataFrame({
    'N Noise Features': [50, 100, 500, 1000, 5000],
    'Tau (vs Augmented)': [np.mean(tau_50_1_2), np.mean(tau_100_1_2), np.mean(tau_500_1_2), np.mean(tau_1000_1_2), np.mean(tau_5000_1_2)],
    'Tau (vs Random)': [np.mean(tau_50_1_3), np.mean(tau_100_1_3), np.mean(tau_500_1_3), np.mean(tau_1000_1_3), np.mean(tau_5000_1_3)],
    'Shapley Magnitude (Orig)': [np.mean(shap_mag_50_orig), np.mean(shap_mag_100_orig), np.mean(shap_mag_500_orig), np.mean(shap_mag_1000_orig), np.mean(shap_mag_5000_orig)],
    'Shapley Magnitude (Aug)': [np.mean(shap_mag_50_aug), np.mean(shap_mag_100_aug), np.mean(shap_mag_500_aug), np.mean(shap_mag_1000_aug), np.mean(shap_mag_5000_aug)]
}, index = [50, 100, 500, 1000, 5000])
```

# Experiments on Variation Across Training Runs
```{python}
# Lets train a bunch of models and see the variance in the results
n_models = 10

joint_models = []
train_accs = []
test_accs = []

for i in range(n_models):
    joint_model = JointMLP(marginal_models=[marg_1, marg_2], hidden_dim=64, dropout=dropout)
    optimizer = AdamW(joint_model.parameters(), lr = 1e-4)
    joint_model, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model, views_orig, y_gt, n_iters = 1000, alpha = None, gamma = 3)
    joint_models.append(joint_model)
    train_accs.append(train_acc)
    test_accs.append(test_acc)
```

```{python}
# get the shapley values for all models:
shap_values = []

for model in joint_models:
    joint_model_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(model)
    explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_wrp, views_orig)
    shap_values.append(explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False))

# get the pairwise weighted tau scores for all models
pairwise_tau = {}

for i, shap_vals1 in enumerate(shap_values):
    for j, shap_vals2 in enumerate(shap_values):
        tau, _ = weightedtau(np.abs(shap_vals1[0][0]).mean(axis=0), np.abs(shap_vals2[0][0]).mean(axis=0))
        pairwise_tau[(i, j)] = tau
```

```{python}
# compare 10 runs where we average over 10 training runs
all_shap_values = []

# 10 runs
n_runs = 10
n_models = 30

for i in range(n_runs):
    joint_models = []
    train_accs = []
    test_accs = []
    shap_values = []

    # for reach run we do the thing where we loop over 10 models, but this time we average their output
    for _ in range(n_models):
        joint_model = JointMLP(marginal_models=[marg_1, marg_2], hidden_dim=64, dropout=dropout)
        optimizer = AdamW(joint_model.parameters(), lr = 1e-4)
        joint_model, train_acc, test_acc = train(joint_model, views_orig, y_gt, n_iters = 500, alpha = None, gamma = 3, prog = False)
        joint_models.append(joint_model)
        train_accs.append(train_acc)
        test_accs.append(test_acc)
    
    # get the shapley values for all models:
    for model in joint_models:
        joint_model_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(model)
        explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_wrp, views_orig)
        shap_values.append(explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False))

    # average the shapley values into a single value
    shap_values = np.median([np.abs(shap_vals[0][0]).mean(axis=0) for shap_vals in shap_values], axis = 0)
    all_shap_values.append(shap_values)

# compute the pairwise tau between all shap values
pairwise_tau = {}

for i, shap_vals1 in enumerate(all_shap_values):
    for j, shap_vals2 in enumerate(all_shap_values):
        tau, _ = weightedtau(shap_vals1, shap_vals2)
        pairwise_tau[(i, j)] = tau
```

### Shapley Values

Shapley values are a method of assigning importance to input variables.  The particular flavor of the Shapley values framework is a version of DEEPLift (Shrikumar, Greenside, and Kundaj, 2017), implemented in the SHAP packages (https://shap.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) and modified with findings from (Lee and Lundberg, 2017).

```{python}
joint_model_wrp = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model)
```

```{python}
# make baselines for the explainer object, 1000 samples centered around the mean
# ACTUALLY: its okay to pass the training data here I think, since there arent that many samples.
# lip_baseline = torch.randn(1000, lip_tensor.shape[1])/5 + lip_tensor.mean().numpy()
# pro_baseline = torch.randn(1000, pro_tensor.shape[1])/5 + pro_tensor.mean().numpy()
# metab_baseline = torch.randn(1000, metab_tensor.shape[1])/5 + metab_tensor.mean().numpy()

# baselines = [lip_baseline, metab_baseline, pro_baseline]
baselines = views_orig

# make the explainer object
explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_wrp, baselines)
```

```{python}
# compute the shapley values
# [lip_baseline, metab_baseline, pro_baseline]
shap_values_orig = explainer.shap_values(views_orig, check_additivity=False)

# shap_save = shap_values_orig

scores_1 = np.abs(shap_values_orig[0][0]).mean(axis=0)
scores_2 = np.abs(shap_save[0][0]).mean(axis=0)

scores_1.argsort()[:20]
scores_2.argsort()[:20]

weightedtau(scores_1, scores_2)
```

# Shapley Values for the augmented data

```{python}
marg_1 = simple_FC(input_size=views_aug[0].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)
marg_2 = simple_FC(input_size=views_aug[1].shape[1], hidden_sizes=[128, 64, last_hidden_size], prediction_dim=ytrain.nunique(), dropout=dropout)

# joint model
joint_model_aug = JointMLP(marginal_models=[marg_1, marg_2], hidden_dim=64, dropout=dropout)

optimizer = AdamW(joint_model_aug.parameters(), lr = 1e-4)
```

```{python}
joint_model_wrp_aug = JointMLPWrapper(joint_model_aug)

# baselines = [lip_baseline, metab_baseline, pro_baseline]
baselines = views_aug

# make the explainer object
explainer = shap.DeepExplainer(joint_model_wrp_aug, baselines)
```

```{python}
# compute the shapley values
# [lip_baseline, metab_baseline, pro_baseline]
shap_values_aug = explainer.shap_values(views_aug, check_additivity=False)
```

```{python}
# compute the shapley values
# Two models
# 1. proteins + metabolites
# 2. proteins + (metabolites + noise)
scores_1 = np.abs(shap_values_orig[0][1]).mean(axis=0)
scores_2 = np.abs(shap_values_aug[0][1]).mean(axis=0)

scores_1.argsort()[:20]
scores_2.argsort()[:20]
```

```{python}
weightedtau(scores_1, scores_2)
```

## Compute Weighted Kendalls Tau between feature rankings of each output

```{python}
# lipids
lipid_corrs = {}

# loop over pairs of cat_names
for i in range(len(cat_names)):
    for j in range(i+1, len(cat_names)):
        # compute the correlation between the shap values
        corr, _ = weightedtau(np.abs(shap_values[i][0]).mean(axis=0), np.abs(shap_values[j][0]).mean(axis=0))
        lipid_corrs[(cat_names[i], cat_names[j])] = corr

# metablites
metab_corrs = {}

# loop over pairs of cat_names
for i in range(len(cat_names)):
    for j in range(i+1, len(cat_names)):
        # compute the correlation between the shap values
        corr, _ = weightedtau(np.abs(shap_values[i][1]).mean(axis=0), np.abs(shap_values[j][1]).mean(axis=0))
        metab_corrs[(cat_names[i], cat_names[j])] = corr

# metablites
protein_corrs = {}

# loop over pairs of cat_names
for i in range(len(cat_names)):
    for j in range(i+1, len(cat_names)):
        # compute the correlation between the shap values
        corr, _ = weightedtau(np.abs(shap_values[i][2]).mean(axis=0), np.abs(shap_values[j][2]).mean(axis=0))
        protein_corrs[(cat_names[i], cat_names[j])] = corr
```

# Sweep Over Noise Levels and Layer Sizes

```{python}
# define noise levels and 
# noise_levels=[100, 500, 1000, 2500, 5000]
noise_levels = [100, 2500, 100, 2500]

multipliers = [np.log2(1 + sz/120) for sz in noise_levels]

hidden_sizes = [
    [[int(m*128), int(m*64)], [128, 16]]
    for m in multipliers[:-1]
]

hidden_sizes.extend([[[128, 64], [512, 128]], [[128, 64], [512, 128]]])
```

```{python}
# get them results!
results = compare_shap_scores(
    train_metab, train_pro, ytrain, 
    noise_levels=noise_levels, 
    hidden_sizes=hidden_sizes,
    n_evals_per_noise=5,
    combine_fn='concat'
)
```

```{python}
tau_2500_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[2500]['results_1_2']]
tau_2500_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[2500]['results_1_3']]

tau_100_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[100]['results_1_2']]
tau_100_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[100]['results_1_3']]

tau_500_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[500]['results_1_2']]
tau_500_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[500]['results_1_3']]

tau_1000_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[1000]['results_1_2']]
tau_1000_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[1000]['results_1_3']]

tau_5000_1_2 = [el[1] for el in results[5000]['results_1_2']]
tau_5000_1_3 = [el[1] for el in results[5000]['results_1_3']]

# the average magnitude of the shapley values from the original model
shap_mag_2500_orig = [np.abs(results[2500]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[2500]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_2500_aug = [np.abs(results[2500]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[2500]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_100_orig = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_100_aug = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_500_orig = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_500_aug = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_1000_orig = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_1000_aug = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_5000_orig = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][0]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_5000_aug = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][0][:, :train_metab.shape[1]]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_2500_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[2500]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[2500]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_2500_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[2500]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[2500]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_100_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_100_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[100]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[100]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_500_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_500_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[500]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[500]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_1000_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_1000_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[1000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[1000]['shap_values_aug']))]

shap_mag_5000_orig_2 = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_orig'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_orig']))]
shap_mag_5000_aug_2 = [np.abs(results[5000]['shap_values_aug'][i][0][1]) for i in range(len(results[5000]['shap_values_aug']))]
```

```{python}
tau_scores, shap_mags = collect_results(results)
```

```{python}
# use reduce to combine the tau_1_3 in the results
all_tau_v_noise = []

for k, v in tau_scores.items():
    all_tau_v_noise += v['tau_1_3']

all_data = [all_tau_v_noise]

for k, v in tau_scores.items():
    all_data.append(v['tau_1_2'])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize = (10, 6))

# make the color the same across all boxplots
sns.boxplot(data = all_data, ax = ax, color = 'skyblue')
ax.set_xticklabels([0] + list(tau_scores.keys()))
ax.set_xlabel('Number of Noise Features')
ax.set_ylabel('Weighted Tau')

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
results_plus = compare_shap_scores(
    train_metab, train_pro, ytrain, 
    noise_levels=[2500], 
    hidden_sizes=[[[569,284], [128, 16]]],
    n_evals_per_noise=5,
    combine_fn='concat'
)

tau_plus, shap_plus = collect_results(results_plus)
```

```{python}
all_tau = {**tau_scores, **tau_plus}

# use reduce to combine the tau_1_3 in the results
all_tau_v_noise = []

for k, v in all_tau.items():
    all_tau_v_noise += v['tau_1_3']

all_data = [all_tau_v_noise]

for k, v in all_tau.items():
    all_data.append(v['tau_1_2'])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize = (10, 6))

# make the color the same across all boxplots
sns.boxplot(data = all_data, ax = ax, color = 'skyblue')
ax.set_xticklabels([0] + list(all_tau.keys()))
ax.set_xlabel('Number of Noise Features')
ax.set_ylabel('Weighted Tau')

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()    
```

```{python}
all_tau_v_noise = tau_100_1_3 + tau_500_1_3 + tau_1000_1_3 + tau_2500_1_3 + tau_5000_1_3

# make some boxplots of the tau values:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize = (10, 6))

# make the color the same across all boxplots
sns.boxplot(data = [all_tau_v_noise, tau_100_1_2, tau_500_1_2, tau_1000_1_2, tau_2500_1_2, tau_5000_1_2], ax = ax, color = 'skyblue')
ax.set_xticklabels([0, 100, 500, 1000, 2500, 5000])
ax.set_xlabel('Number of Noise Features')
ax.set_ylabel('Weighted Tau')

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# define noise levels and subsequently layer sizes based on a multiplier
orig_sizes = [[64, 64], [512, 512]]

# the original ratio between input sizes is....
p1_size = train_metab.shape[1]
p2_size = train_pro.shape[1]
orig_ratio = train_metab.shape[1]/train_pro.shape[1]

n_noise = [100, 500, 1000, 2500, 4000]
layer_sizes = [orig_sizes] * 5

mults = []

```

```{python}
# Scheme 1
for nl in n_noise:
    # compute the new input size
    mult = (1 + nl/p1_size)**5

    mults.append(mult)

    new_sizes = orig_sizes.copy()

    # new_x_solver = -tensor_1.shape[1] + np.sqrt()

    new_sizes[0] = [int(sz * mult ** 0.5) for sz in new_sizes[0]]
    new_sizes[1] = [int(sz / mult ** 0.5) for sz in new_sizes[1]]

    layer_sizes.append(new_sizes)

n_noise = n_noise * 2
```

```{python}
# define noise levels and subsequently layer sizes based on a multiplier
orig_sizes = [[64, 64], [512, 512]]

# the original ratio between input sizes is....
p1_size = train_metab.shape[1]
p2_size = train_pro.shape[1]
orig_ratio = train_metab.shape[1]/train_pro.shape[1]

n_noise = [100, 500, 1000, 2500, 4000]
layer_sizes = [orig_sizes] * 5

mults = []

for nl in n_noise:
    # compute the new input size
    mult = (1 + nl/p1_size)**0.5

    mults.append(mult)

    new_sizes = orig_sizes.copy()

    jmodel, _ = make_joint_model(
        input_size = [train_metab.shape[1], train_pro.shape[1]], 
        hidden_sizes = orig_sizes,
        combine_fn = "concat"
    )

    # get number of parameters in jmodel:
    n_params_0 = sum(p.numel() for p in jmodel.margin_models[0].parameters())
    n_params_1 = sum(p.numel() for p in jmodel.margin_models[1].parameters())

    # a = 1, b = (1 + d_in), c = mult * n_params, solve for x with quadratic formula:
    # x = (-b + sqrt(b^2 - 4ac))/2a
    x_0 = (-1 + np.sqrt(1 + 4 * mult * n_params_0))/2

    # now with c = n_params/mult
    x_1 = (-1 + np.sqrt(1 + 4 * n_params_1/mult))/2

    jmodel, _ = make_joint_model(
        input_size = [train_metab.shape[1] + nl, train_pro.shape[1]], 
        hidden_sizes = [[int(x_0)]*2, [int(x_1)]*2],
        combine_fn = "concat"
    )

    new_sizes[0] = [int(x_0)]*2
    new_sizes[1] = [int(x_1)]*2

    layer_sizes.append(new_sizes)

n_noise = n_noise * 2
```

```{python}
# get them results!
results_squeeze = compare_shap_scores(
    train_metab, train_pro, ytrain, 
    noise_levels=n_noise, 
    hidden_sizes=layer_sizes,
    n_evals_per_noise=5,
    combine_fn='concat',
    test_x = test_x,
    test_y = test_y,
    prog=False
)

thetime = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d")

pickle.dump(results_squeeze, open(f"/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/squeeze_tau_results_{thetime}.pkl", 'wb'))
```

```{python}
# plot the data, optionally load previous results
# results_squeeze = pickle.load(open('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/squeeze_tau_results_2024-10-15.pkl', 'rb'))

tau_squeeze, shap_squeeze = collect_results(results_squeeze)

# rename the keys of tau_squeeze
new_tau = {}

for (i, k1), m in zip(enumerate(tau_squeeze.keys()), mults * 2):
    if i < 5:
        new_k = n_noise[i]
    else:
        new_k = (n_noise[i], "{:.2f}".format(m))

    new_tau[new_k] = tau_squeeze[k1]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize = (10, 6))

plot_tau(new_tau, ax)

ax.set_xlabel('Number of noise features, multiplier')
# rotate
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig('/Users/clab683/git_repos/DeepIMV/output/layer_size_experiments/images/fixed_vs_variable_layer_tau.png', dpi = 300)
```

```{python}
def print_auc(results):
    for k, v in results.items():
        auc_aug = v['auc_aug']
        auc_orig = v['auc_orig']
        print(f"{k}, original auc: {auc_orig}, augmented auc {auc_aug}")
```

```{python}
model_sizes = []

for ls, nl in zip(layer_sizes, n_noise):
    # get the number of parameters in the model for these values:

    jm, opt = make_joint_model(
        input_size = [train_metab.shape[1] + nl, train_pro.shape[1]], 
    )

    n_params = sum(p.numel() for p in jm.parameters())

    model_sizes.append(n_params)
```
